steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Rewrite the quadratic to identify the vertex value:
$$f(x)=x^2-12x+30$$
$$f(x)=(x^2-12x+36)-36+30$$
$$f(x)=(x-6)^2-6$$

steps.title: Read the minimum value
steps.content: The squared term $(x-6)^2$ is never negative, so the least possible value occurs when $(x-6)^2=0$. At that point, the function value is $-6$. The answer is B.
